headline: Golf Tournament Decides To Replace Balls With Living Things, Tournament Becomes Ethical Nightmare
summary: The 'Humane Open' uses hamsters instead of golf balls, with critics calling it 'too chaotic' and supporters calling it 'finally interesting.'
fullBody: The Annual Stuffy Golf Club has introduced a revolutionary new format for their tournament: replacing traditional golf balls with actual living hamsters. The 'Humane Open,' as they've branded it, requires players to gently tap hamsters toward the hole using specially designed clubs with padding. Players are not allowed to pick up the hamsters, force them in any direction, or say anything negative about their putting performance. The tournament has become an ethical nightmare, with spectators arguing about whether the hamsters are 'having fun' or 'experiencing golf-based trauma.' One hamster reportedly ran into a water hazard and was disqualified for 'going off course.' Another hamster, named 'Mr. Whiskers,' has become the tournament favorite and has his own fan club with 47,000 members. Animal rights activists have filed 300 lawsuits, while golf enthusiasts say 'finally, golf is interesting again.'
imagePrompt: Golf players tapping hamsters gently with padded clubs, confused spectators arguing, hamster wearing tiny golf helmet, comic illustration, flat design, green and brown colors
